steps.title: Divide the polynomials
steps.content: Divide $x^2-2x-5$ by $x-3$. The first term is $x$ because $x^2\div x=x$. Subtracting $x(x-3)=x^2-3x$ leaves $x-5$.

steps.title: Finish the remainder
steps.content: Next, $xdiv x=1$. Subtracting ${1}(x-3)=x-3$ leaves a remainder of $-2$. Therefore $$\frac{x^2-2x-5}{x-3}=x+1-\frac{2}{x-3}.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: That confirms D. The negative remainder matters: since the remainder is $-2$, the final term is $-\frac{2}{x-3}$, not $+\frac{2}{x-3}$.
